reference_postmortem: At T-06:00, payments-api was updated to v2.3.2 which changed the total_amount column precision from NUMERIC(12,4) to NUMERIC(12,2), causing silent truncation of order totals. For example, an order of $49.9975 would be stored as $49.99, losing $0.0075 per transaction. At T+00:00 (episode start), a scheduled VACUUM ANALYZE on postgres-replica caused a CPU spike — this was a red herring unrelated to the silent data corruption. Root cause: payments-api v2.3.2 decimal serialization bug. Mitigation: rollback payments-api to v2.3.0. Critical follow-up: conduct a full data audit of all orders created between the v2.3.2 deployment and the rollback to identify and correct truncated amounts; implement schema validation tests in CI/CD.
